definition: to give up completely and leave something; to cease to support or protect someone or something; to leave or forsake something, such as a person, place, or cause.
example: The family abandoned their old home.

definition: to take in and hold all of someone's energy or attention; to soak up something, especially liquid; to become fully engaged in an activity.
to take in and be able to understand or handle a situation, information, etc.; to take charge or control of something.
positioned higher than someone or something else; not on the same level as another person or thing; situated at a higher place or position.
example: The ball is above the net.

definition: to be with someone or something as they go somewhere; to travel together with someone or something.
example: I'll accompany you to the event.

definition: a mutual understanding or agreement; a formal agreement reached by negotiation; an arrangement by which two or more parties agree to cooperate in order to achieve a common goal.
example: We could not come to an accord.

definition: to agree with someone about something; to accept or allow what someone else suggests or does
example: They didn't want to leave early, but I finally acced to their request.
It is important for everyone to be in agreement with the plan.
